provenance.source_corpus: Fertilization at planting influences seedling growth and vegetative competition on a post-mining boreal reclamation site

source_spans.text: Fertilization at planting influences seedling growth and vegetative competition on a post-mining boreal reclamation site
Fertilization at planting influences seedling growth and vegetative competition on a post-mining boreal reclamation site
Fertilization at planting influences seedling growth and vegetative competition on a post-mining boreal reclamation site
IAF and IA-L treatments resulted in substantial increases in competing vegetation cover
No effect on seedling survival occurred.
Directed application of controlled-release fertilizer (CRF) to the rhizosphere offers an alternative
broadcast fertilization with immediately available fertilizers (IAF) results in generally low rates of nutrient recovery
White spruce year-1 height and diameter growth responded positively to both IAF and CRF
Aspen height growth did not respond strongly to fertilization in either year, but diameter growth showed positive responses
CRF promoted seedling growth and nutrient uptake
